name: Rahul Krishnan
title: Data Science Master's Student
subtitle: MSc Data Science student at Universitaet Trier with approximately 4 years of software engineering experience at SAP Labs. Working across LLMs, recommender systems, graph ML, and MLOps.
currentRole: MSc Data Science at Universitaet Trier

paragraphs: I'm Rahul Krishnan, a Data Science master's student at Universitaet Trier and a former SAP software developer with around four years of backend engineering behind me. I build AI systems end to end, from training and evaluation through to containerised deployment, and I am drawn to the engineering layer that decides whether a model actually ships: latency, memory, cost and reliability.

My MSc thesis tackles a core bottleneck in large-language-model inference, the memory the KV cache consumes at long context: it finds a 2x-3x compression 'free zone' that holds generation quality, confirmed on downstream reasoning and retrieval rather than perplexity alone (the method itself is unpublished for now). Around it I have studied where LLMs can be trusted, from chain-of-thought faithfulness on BIG-Bench Hard to reward design for calibration and selective abstention. On the applied side I have shipped a RAG assistant on Google Cloud Run, a Two-Tower recommender on Azure, and a neuro-symbolic model for cross-lingual narrative similarity.

I care about results that hold up: multi-seed evaluation, honest error bars and measured latency over single-run headline numbers. My interests sit at the intersection of LLM inference efficiency, evaluation and alignment, recommender systems, and reliable MLOps.
